entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Tetraploid oil and food legume, scientific name Arachis hypogaea

entities.name: SEED OIL CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of oil present in peanut seeds

entities.name: PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of protein present in peanut seeds

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCE
entities.description: A high-quality sequence of the peanut genome comprising 2.54 Gb

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the peanut seed

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the peanut plant to resist diseases

entities.name: SYMBIOTIC NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process by which peanut plants fix nitrogen through symbiosis

entities.name: B SUBGENOME
entities.description: One of the two subgenomes in the tetraploid peanut genome, characterized by more genes and expression dominance

entities.name: A SUBGENOME
entities.description: One of the two subgenomes in the tetraploid peanut genome, associated with long-terminal-repeat expansion

entities.name: LONG-TERMINAL-REPEAT EXPANSION
entities.description: The expansion of long-terminal-repeat elements within the A subgenome

entities.name: POLYPLOID GENOME
entities.description: A genome containing multiple sets of chromosomes, providing insight into legume evolution

entities.name: RESEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of sequencing multiple accessions to study genetic variation and domestication

entities.name: DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of adapting wild peanut species for cultivation

entities.name: POLYPLOIDY
entities.description: The state of having more than two paired sets of chromosomes

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: The diversity in genetic sequences among individuals of a species

entities.name: MAPPING
entities.description: The process of identifying the chromosomal location of specific traits or genes

entities.name: CANDIDATE-GENE DISCOVERY
entities.description: The process of identifying genes that are likely responsible for a specific trait

entities.name: SEED COLOR
entities.description: The pigmentation of the peanut seed

entities.name: FOLIAR DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the peanut plant to resist diseases affecting the foliage

entities.name: FUNCTIONAL GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of gene function and interaction

entities.name: PEANUT IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The application of genomic data to enhance peanut crop characteristics
